attributes.content:
# Sticky Note — Visual Design

Guidance for making the card actually look like a sticky note.

## Color Palette

Use paper-like, slightly muted colors. The card should evoke a physical Post-it note.

| Name   | Background  | Accent / shadow |
| ------ | ----------- | --------------- |
| yellow | `#fff6a8`   | `#e6d268`       |
| pink   | `#fdc4d6`   | `#e893ad`       |
| blue   | `#bde0fe`   | `#7eb8ed`       |
| green  | `#c9f5d0`   | `#7fc991`       |
| orange | `#ffd6a5`   | `#eaa760`       |
| purple | `#e1c8f5`   | `#b48be0`       |

Default color is `yellow`.

## Layout

- **isolated** — large square card (~360px on a side), title (if any) bold near the top, body wraps below in a hand-written-feeling sans-serif. Add a subtle `box-shadow` and a slight `transform: rotate(-1deg)` to suggest a physical note.
- **embedded** — fixed aspect square preview (200×200) showing color and the first line of body text.
- **fitted** — fills available space, no rotation (so it composes cleanly into a grid), shows up to ~3 lines of body text with ellipsis.

## Typography

A system sans-serif is fine (`-apple-system, Segoe UI, sans-serif`). Body should be at least 14px in `fitted` and 18px in `isolated`.

## Accessibility

Ensure foreground text remains readable on every background color — always render body text in dark gray (`#222`) rather than pure black, and never rely on color alone to communicate meaning.
